rationale: Luo et al. is cited in the core model-development discussion, where the authors define prior quantitative models that use subjective characteristics and then position their framework against that approach. The citing paper explicitly argues its method is fundamentally different because it does not require a priori specification and direct ratings of such higher-order constructs, and instead infers latent goals from choice. This is a meaningful framing/positioning role, even though the paper does not directly reuse Luo et al.'s estimation procedure or run a direct empirical comparison to it.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Luo et al. (2008) as a representative prior subjective-characteristics modeling approach and contrasts it with its own bottom-up latent-goal, multiobjective model. The citation is used to motivate the conceptual distinction and claimed contribution of the new framework.
